prose:
Computed Fukui indices for formaldehyde

Method-provenance block
Property: Condensed Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and dual descriptor (per atom)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (4 atoms); single-point charges on this fixed gas-phase geometry, no further optimisation (DFT//input geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation/anion states run as doublets (±1 charge, multiplicity 2)
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`, PySCF backend)
Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations (N, N+1, N–1) converged; integrity.trustworthy = true. Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key results (Mulliken scheme)
Atoms (1-based indexing): 1 O, 2 C, 3 H, 4 H
f⁺ = [0.46813824, 0.70839130, -0.088269392, -0.088260145]
f⁻ = [0.46742464, 0.11244422,  0.21007563,  0.21005551]
f⁰ = [0.46778144, 0.41041776,  0.060903121, 0.060897683]
Dual descriptor Δf = [ 0.00071359555, 0.59594709, -0.29834503, -0.29831566]

Most electrophilic site: atom 2 (C) with f⁺ = 0.70839130
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.46742464

Live run log: the calculation streamed to a `fukui_<timestamp>.out` file in the current working directory (announced at launch); it contains full PySCF SCF cycles and optimizer output for the N, N+1, and N-1 single-point evaluations.

Warnings (verbatim from engine)
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

The headline numeric value reported above (0.7083913) is the largest condensed electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) located on the carbon atom (index 2).
